title: podman run - avoid calls to JSONDeepCopy
body:
A series of commits to avoid all calls to JSONDeepCopy when running a simple `podman run --rm -d busybox true`.  After https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11768/commits/5d6ea90e756cd48c90d9549d25c0150c9bb5c50c this drops another 100kB of memory usage.

Please refer to the individual commits for change descriptions.

Note, this needs a head nod from @mheon.

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Is there reason why you closed the `copier.Copy()` instead of `JSONDeepCopy()` PR, this should still help for the case were we need a deep copy.

> Is there reason why you closed the `copier.Copy()` instead of `JSONDeepCopy()` PR, this should still help for the case were we need a deep copy.

Based on your and @mheon's feedback, I decided to first optimize the code and then take another look, if needed, at optimizing JSONDeepCopy.  @mheon pointed out https://github.com/kubernetes/gengo/tree/master/examples/deepcopy-gen which may be a nice alternative.
quay.io looks flaky at the moment.
